name: canopy CLI surface
description: Warren's canopy facade shells out to two cn subcommands: 'cn list --tag agent --json' (returns the {success, command, prompts:[{name,version,status,tags}]} envelope filtered to active prompts) and 'cn render <name> --json' (returns {success, command, name, version, sections:[{name,body}], resolvedFrom, frontmatter}). The global --json flag is the right one — 'cn render --format json' emits a bare object without success/command, which makes auth-vs-failure disambiguation lossy. Failed renders return {success:false, command:'render', error:<msg>} on stdout AND exit 1; the warren facade peeks for the structured error envelope before falling back to exit-code interpretation.

title: Filter agents by canopy tag 'agent', not by frontmatter
rationale: Warren SPEC §2.1 wording is 'agent: true schema tag' which reads ambiguous. Canopy supports both freeform tags (string[]) and frontmatter (Record<string, unknown>); 'cn list --tag agent --json' is the supported CLI filter and matches the example in canopy SPEC.md line 73 ('tags: ["agent", "leaf"]'). Picking tag-based filtering keeps warren aligned with the canopy CLI surface — operators use 'cn create --tag agent' to register an agent, not a frontmatter field.

content: Required sections for warren's agent definition schema: 'system' is the only required section. Optional sections from SPEC §4.2 (skills, expertise_seed, burrow_config, workflow) are consumed at run-spawn time (Phase 5) — Phase 3's schema does not require them. Canopy's per-prompt --schema rules are the right place to enforce richer structural constraints; warren only enforces the minimum needed to spawn an agent.

name: Per-agent error isolation in registry refresh
description: registry.refreshAgentRegistry collects per-prompt failures into RefreshResult.skipped instead of throwing — one bad prompt (missing 'system' section, archived between list and render, etc.) shouldn't block the other 19 from registering. Transport-layer failures (cn binary missing, canopy clone unreachable) DO abort the whole refresh because there's nothing useful to partially register if the registry is unreadable. Mirrors the burrow-client transport-error mapping pattern: per-call structured errors flow through; per-channel failures throw CanopyUnavailableError.

title: Hard-reset to origin/HEAD on every refresh, no merge
rationale: Warren's canopy clone is a cache, not a working tree. Operators edit prompts in their own checkout, push to GitHub, then refresh. A regular 'git pull' can fail with conflicts if anything stray landed in the warren-side clone (half-finished cn write, file-mode drift), so cloneOrUpdateCanopyRepo does 'git fetch --prune' + 'git reset --hard origin/<HEAD>'. Default branch is auto-detected via 'git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD' with a 'git remote set-head --auto' fallback, so most repos work without configuration.

content: Warren env vars for canopy registry are warren-namespaced AND keep CANOPY_REPO_URL un-prefixed: CANOPY_REPO_URL (the operator's library URL — required, no default), WARREN_CANOPY_DIR (local clone path, default /data/canopy-repo), WARREN_CN_BINARY (default 'cn'), WARREN_GIT_BINARY (default 'git'). CANOPY_REPO_URL is the canonical name in SPEC §10.2 and matches how the operator sets it via 'fly secrets set CANOPY_REPO_URL=...' — keep it un-prefixed even though warren-side configs are normally WARREN_*.

Canopy claude-code prompt (canopy-daf3) needs an explicit commit clause: 'commit your changes; warren handles the push, you do not need to push it yourself'. Validated 2026-05-09 (run_agpet4ev6e4a): adding the clause produced ahead_by: 1 with a real diff. Do NOT instruct the agent to push — agent-side git push fails inside the sandbox (warren-1a09: /root/.gitconfig invisible to bwrap, GITHUB_TOKEN not env-passthroughed). Warren reap is the actual push mechanism (host-side, sees /root/.gitconfig). Without the commit clause, runs silently no-op (run_a98cfx1fantf: 49 turns, ahead_by: 0, branchPushed: true).

canopy claude-code (canopy-daf3) is now the sandbox operating contract: workspace map (.canopy/agent.json, .mulch/expertise/*.jsonl, .seeds/issues.jsonl), rituals (ml prime, sd ready/close, quality gates, ml record/sd close at terminal turn), steering protocol ([STEERING] user turns are priority), expectations (no opportunistic refactors, never --no-verify, network governed by burrow_config, commit-only — warren reaps and pushes per warren-1a09). The canopy library lives at github.com/jayminwest/understory.git (renamed from my-canopy).
